summaryText: Pastor Darrison Tellez opened with a warm welcome and shared a personal story about receiving unexpected help with a flat tire in Wyoming, illustrating that we often face struggles we were not meant to handle alone. He introduced the sermon's theme, 'Help I Need Some Backup,' from Joshua 10, where the Gibeonites call on Joshua for aid against their enemies. Pastor Darrison taught that God gives His people the outcome of victory before the battle even begins, as He told Joshua not to be afraid because the victory was already secured. He emphasized that God is not bound by time and speaks to our future as if it has already happened, encouraging us to fight from a place of victory, not for it. The message highlighted that God fights harder for us than we fight for ourselves, using the hailstorm in Joshua's battle as a metaphor for God's unseen, detailed intervention in our lives. Pastor Darrison shared a powerful testimony of God's protective hand during a traumatic mall shooting, affirming that God is always at work in the details, even when we cannot see Him. He challenged the congregation to pray bigger, 'impossible' prayers, like Joshua's prayer for the sun to stand still, connecting the finality of our fights to the level of our faith. The sermon concluded with an invitation to accept Jesus as the ultimate backup and source of salvation, celebrating those who made that decision.
mainPoints: God gives us the assurance of victory before the battle begins, calling us to fight from a place of confidence in His promises.
God is actively fighting for us in unseen ways, intervening in the details of our lives more than we realize.
Our faith is demonstrated and strengthened when we pray bold, 'impossible' prayers, trusting in God's power to overcome any obstacle.

versesMentioned.text: The men of Gibeon quickly sent messengers to Joshua at his camp at Gilgal. 'Do not abandon your servants,' they pleaded. 'Come up to us quickly and save us! Help us, because all the Amorite kings from the hill country have joined forces against us.'
The Lord said to Joshua, 'Do not be afraid of them; I have given them into your hand. Not one of them will be able to withstand you.'

As the Amorites retreated down the road from Beth Horon, the Lord hurled large hailstones down on them from the sky, all the way to Azekah, and they died. More died from the hail than were killed by the swords of the Israelites.
The Lord makes firm the steps of the one who delights in him;

Monday.scripture: Joshua 10:8 - 'Do not be afraid of them; I have given them into your hand.'
Monday.action: Identify one worry or challenge you are facing. Write down the promise that God has already secured the victory in this area.
Monday.prayer: Lord, thank you that the battle is already won. Help me to face today from a place of victory, not fear.

Tuesday.scripture: Psalm 37:23 - 'The Lord makes firm the steps of the one who delights in him;'
Tuesday.action: Look back on your week so far and write down one 'coincidence' or small blessing. Acknowledge it as God's detailed care for you.
Tuesday.prayer: Father, open my eyes to see your hand at work in the small details of my life today. I trust you are fighting for me.

Wednesday.scripture: Joshua 10:11 - '...the Lord hurled large hailstones down on them from the sky...'
Wednesday.action: In a quiet moment, reflect on a past difficulty. Consider one way God might have been protecting you or working behind the scenes that you didn't see at the time.
Wednesday.prayer: God, even when I don't see you, I believe you are working. Thank you for fighting battles for me that I am unaware of.

Thursday.scripture: Joshua 10:12-13 - '...Joshua said to the Lord... 'Sun, stand still over Gibeon...''
Thursday.action: Pray one 'impossible' prayer today. Ask God for something that feels too big for you, trusting in His power, not your own.
Thursday.prayer: Jesus, I bring my biggest need to you today. I believe you are the God of the impossible. I ask for your intervention with bold faith.

Friday.scripture: Romans 8:37 - '...we are more than conquerors through him who loved us.'
Friday.action: Speak this truth over yourself: 'I am more than a conqueror through Christ.' Repeat it whenever you feel challenged today.
Friday.prayer: Holy Spirit, remind me of my true identity in Christ. I am not a victim of my circumstances; I am a victorious child of God.

Saturday.scripture: John 3:16 - 'For God so loved the world that he gave his one and only Son, that whoever believes in him shall not perish but have eternal life.'
Saturday.action: If you have never accepted Jesus, take a moment to pray the prayer of salvation. If you have, share with one person why Jesus is your source of help and hope.
Saturday.prayer: Jesus, I need you as my Savior and my backup. I give my life to you. Thank you for fighting for me and saving me.
